name: WordPress.com REST API
what_it_does: WordPress.com hosted platform REST API — enabling developers to programmatically manage WordPress.com blogs and sites, publish posts, manage media, handle comments, manage users, and access site statistics, with OAuth2 authentication, supporting both WordPress.com hosted sites and Jetpack-connected self-hosted WordPress.org sites through a unified API surface.
use_cases: Publishing posts programmatically via WordPress.com API for content automation pipelines
Managing media uploads via WordPress.com API for automated image publishing workflows
Retrieving site statistics via WordPress.com Stats API for analytics dashboards
Syndicating content via WordPress.com API for multi-platform publishing
Managing Jetpack-connected WordPress.org sites via WordPress.com API for unified management
not_for: Self-hosted WordPress.org without Jetpack (use WordPress REST API v2 built into WordPress core)
Enterprise WordPress with custom plugins that need direct DB access
best_when: A developer needs to manage WordPress.com hosted sites programmatically, or control Jetpack-connected self-hosted WordPress sites through a unified API — particularly for content publishing automation.
avoid_when: You're running self-hosted WordPress.org without Jetpack — use the built-in WordPress REST API (wp-json) instead.

auth.notes: OAuth2 authorization code flow for user-delegated access. Application passwords for server-to-server. OAuth2 scopes: global (full access), posts, comments, likes, follow, stats. Client credentials via application ID and secret. JWT tokens available. Webhook subscriptions for real-time events. API base: public-api.wordpress.com/rest/v1.1. Site ID required for all site-specific operations.

pricing.free_tier_limits: WordPress.com free plan: 1GB storage, wordpress.com subdomain; API access included
pricing.paid_tiers: Personal $4/month; Premium $8/month; Business $25/month; Commerce $45/month (all with API access)
pricing.notes: Automattic (San Francisco). Founded by Matt Mullenweg. WordPress.com is managed WordPress hosting. WordPress.org is the open-source software. 43% of all websites run WordPress (combined). WordPress.com hosts 60M+ sites. Jetpack plugin connects self-hosted WordPress.org to WordPress.com API ecosystem. Stats API provides rich traffic analytics. WooCommerce on Business+ plans. Calypso is Automattic's JavaScript client using this API. REST API distinct from WordPress.org REST API (wp-json). Site ID: numeric ID found in site settings or via /me/sites endpoint. WordPress.com VIP for enterprise ($2K+/month).

performance.rate_limits: 5,000 requests per hour per access token

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: OAuth2 with scopes. Site ID required for all calls. 5,000 req/hr rate limit. Automattic (US). GDPR compliant. Jetpack extends to self-hosted WordPress. REST API v1.1 (not same as WordPress core REST API).
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Post reads idempotent by post ID. Site reads idempotent by site ID. Post creation: not idempotent — check for slug before creating. Media uploads: not idempotent. Update operations use POST (not idempotent) or PUT.
known_agent_gotchas: WordPress.com API ≠ WordPress.org REST API — different endpoints and auth mechanisms
Site ID required: numeric ID (e.g., 12345) or site domain (mysite.wordpress.com)
Jetpack required for self-hosted WordPress.org sites to use this API
OAuth2 redirect flow required for user tokens — no simple API key for user operations
Rate limit: 5,000 req/hr per access token — shared across all operations
Post status: publish vs draft vs private — explicit status required when creating posts

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. OAuth2 with granular scopes. Automattic security team. GDPR compliant. SOC2 certified. User blog content and personal data stored on Automattic servers. WordPress.com VIP for enterprise security requirements. OAuth2 application review process for production apps.
